input.command: where.exe codebase-memory-mcp 2>$null; Get-Command codebase-memory-mcp -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object -ExpandProperty Source

input.description: Index Elay Backend repository into knowledge graph
Check index status by project name
Check git status of codebase-memory artifacts
Check graph artifact file timestamp and size
Verify indexed graph with architecture summary

content.text:
Indexing is complete. The knowledge graph for **Elay Backend** has been refreshed.

## Index results

| Metric | Before | After |
|--------|--------|-------|
| **Nodes** | 9,924 | **10,365** (+441) |
| **Edges** | 22,479 | **23,928** (+1,449) |
| **Files scanned** | — | 1,319 |
| **Files changed** | — | 135 (incremental) |
| **Status** | — | `ready` |

## What was indexed

The incremental update picked up recent work, especially the **Chat module**:

- Domain entities (`ChatConversation`, `ChatMessage`, `ChatParticipant`, etc.)
- CQRS handlers and commands (send message, conversations, mark as read, etc.)
- Carter endpoints under `Customer/Api/EndPoint/Chat/`
- SignalR `ChatHub` and `ChatNotificationService`
- EF migrations and configurations
- Updates to `FormDataItemQueryHandler.cs`

## Note

The indexer logged `artifact.export err=write_artifact`, so `.codebase-memory/graph.db.zst` in the repo may not have been updated on disk. The MCP server index itself is ready and queryable (`index_status` returns `ready`).

If you want the committed artifact refreshed for teammates, we can troubleshoot the export write error next. Otherwise you can use `search_graph`, `get_architecture`, `trace_path`, and the other codebase-memory MCP tools against the updated graph.

input.description: Get artifact file timestamps and sizes

content.text:
The indexing run finished successfully.

**MCP index:** `D-Elay_Backend-master` is `ready` with **10,365 nodes** and **23,928 edges** (up from 9,924 / 22,479).

**Repo artifact:** `.codebase-memory/graph.db.zst` and `artifact.json` were **not** updated — they still show **July 1** timestamps and the old counts. Git is clean, which matches the `artifact.export err=write_artifact` error during indexing.

The live MCP graph is current and usable. The committed artifact teammates would pull is still stale. I can troubleshoot the artifact export write failure next if you want that fixed.

[REDACTED]
